title: expose default network name in podman info
body:
### Feature request description

The default network is called podman as default. However that can be changed in containers.conf, some users like to know what the default is, see https://github.com/podman-desktop/podman-desktop/issues/14816

### Suggest potential solution

Expose the default network name in podman info, I think we can add it as field under `networkBackendInfo`

### Have you considered any alternatives?

A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.

### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body:
hello do you think we can get that information with the network REST API as well ? 
Or it would be part of the /info endpoint

That should be part of the libpod/info endpoint if we put under podman info.

If you like a specific API endpoint to only ask for the default network name it would seem a bit overkill for me but certainly not something I would object too if you prefer that.

> If you like a specific API endpoint to only ask for the default network name it would seem a bit overkill for me but certainly not something I would object too if you prefer that.

I don't think we need this. However it would be useful to have a property on the Network Inspect Object, like `isDefault: boolean`.

But I think we would be to deal with the `/info` 👍

Moreover, looking at the libpod/info api

```
$:  ./bin/podman system service --time=0 tcp::localhost:8888
```

Then curling `/info` did not provide the default network, do you think can be addressed as a separate issue?

> Moreover, looking at the libpod/info api
> 
> ```
> $:  ./bin/podman system service --time=0 tcp::localhost:8888
> ```
> 
> Then curling `/info` did not provide the default network, do you think can be addressed as a separate issue?

Creating a separate issue would also be fine.
If you open one, I’ll take care of the related work as well.

>> Great! But the default_network is using snake_case when all other property in the json is using camelCase, is this expected?
> I think I may have missed this part. I’ll need to submit a follow-up PR to fix it.
Would it be okay if I handle this in a new PR?

Sure file a new PR to change it, given there are no releases with that yet we are free to change as we like

> Then curling /info did not provide the default network, do you think can be addressed as a separate issue?

`/info` is a docker compatible endpoint, it can only contain info docker provides so unless they have a field for that info then no we cannot add it.

Mistake on my side, I tried the endpoint `/v4.2.0/libpod/info` and I can successfully see the `default_network` 🚀  !

Let's rename this to `defaultNetwork` for consistency before we start implementing code consuming this property
